title: A CPU Made of Atoms: IBM's Breakthrough 0.7nm Transistors
author: Daniel Nenni
text:
Daniel Nenni
Founder
Staff member

IBM reckons Moore's Law isn't finished, and their new 7A Nanostack is how they're proving it. Ask Nvidia and they'll tell you scaling is dead. Ask TSMC and they don't seem to care either way. IBM lands firmly on yes, scaling still works.

In this video I go through what 7A Nanostack is and how it works. We start with the basics of a transistor, cover the shift from planar to FinFET to gate-all-around, then get to how IBM stacks an NMOS and a PMOS transistor on top of each other in what the industry calls a complementary FET, or CFET.

Papers:
Reboh et al, NanoStack Transistor Architecture for CMOS 7A Node and Beyond, VLSI 2025: https://dx.doi.org/10.23919/VLSITechn...
Zhang et al, Area and Performance of Staggered-Channel Nanostack SRAM Bitcells, VLSI 2026: https://dx.doi.org/10.1109/VLSITechno...
Substack: https://morethanmoore.substack.com/p/...
IBM announcement: https://newsroom.ibm.com/2026-06-25-i...

The companies that fall under this banner include AMD, Applied Materials, Arm, Armari, ASM, Ayar Labs, Baidu, Bolt Graphics, Dialectica, Facebook, GLG, Guidepoint, IBM, Impala, Infineon, Intel, Kuehne+Nagel, Lattice Semi, Linode, MediaTek, NeuReality, NextSilicon, NordPass, NVIDIA, ProteanTecs, Qualcomm, Recogni, SiFive, SIG, SiTime, Supermicro, Synopsys, Tenstorrent, Third Bridge, TSMC, Untether AI, Ventana Micro.
